title: uputregf
text: uputregf Copy a double-precision floating-point value into the specified general variable ($1 ... $99). Explain this code long uputregf (shortregNbr, doublevalue); Copy Parameters regNbr Number of the Uniface general variable. This must be from 1 to 99. value Double-precision floating point value to put into the general variable. Return Values 0— uputregf always returns zero. Use Library: yrtl Description uputregf copies the double-precision floating-point value to the Uniface general variable (also known as dollar register) with the specified number value , which can range from 1 to 99. There is no check on the validity of value ; if it is out of range, a crash is likely to result. Note that the precision of a double value is system-dependent, and might not match the 24-digit internal precision of Uniface. You might see truncation or rounding problems when you use too many significant digits. Uniface will always format general variables with double values in scientific format, even if value is not. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> EXPORT long putdoubles () { uputregf(1, -1.23E-10); /* $1 is displayed in Uniface as -1.23000000000000e-010 */ uputregf(2, 4.5678E20); /* $2 is displayed in Uniface as 4.56780000000000e+020 */ uputregf(3, 123.456); /* $3 is displayed in Uniface as 1.23456000000000e+002 */ } Copy Related Links ugetregf Get the double-precision value of a Uniface general variable ($1 ... $99). uputreg Copy a long integer value into the specified general variable ($1 ... $99). uputregs Copy a string value to the specified general variable ($1 ... $99).
